name: Hawaiian Fruit Bread-Oldie
recipeInstructions:
1. Heat the pineapple juice and add the chopped dates, stirring occasionally until the mixture thickens.
2. Cool the mixture.
3. Cream together the honey and butter (or shortening) and beat in the egg.
4. Add the date mixture and nut meats to the creamed mixture.
5. Sift together the dry ingredients and add them to the creamed mixture along with the dates.
6. Pour the batter into a well-greased loaf pan (10x4x3 inches), with the bottom lined with wax paper to prevent sticking.
7. Bake in a moderately slow oven (325°F) for one hour.
8. Allow the bread to cool before slicing. It will slice better if left to sit overnight and used the next day.
